title: Helleborine ovata
intro: Helleborine ovata, also known as the ovate helleborine or green helleborine, is a type of orchid native to Asia and Europe, characterized by its greenish-yellow flowers and ovate-shaped leaves. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help to alleviate symptoms of respiratory issues and protect against cell damage. Bioactive constituents of Helleborine ovata include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and other compounds that contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Helleborine ovata is often used to make herbal preparations such as infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are used to treat a variety of health conditions, including respiratory and cardiovascular issues.
taxonomy: Helleborine ovata is a species of orchid native to Asia, and it belongs to the genus Helleborine, which is part of the subtribe Orchideae within the family Orchidaceae. The Orchideae subtribe is further classified into several tribes, including the Neottieae and Orchideae, which both contain species of orchids.
